Plan: new internal/progress package (events.go, sink.go, pane.go, log.go, snapshot.go, term.go), funnel Options gains Progress sink emitting at run/stage/agent/spend/budget boundaries, daemon Deps gains Progress sink for cycle/schedule/reverify/promote, CLI wires PaneRenderer (TTY) / LogRenderer (non-TTY) + Snapshot for scan, LogRenderer-over-slog + Snapshot for daemon, new 'bugbot status' command reading status.json.

comments.author: Dustin
comments.text: Implementation complete. New package internal/progress (events.go, sink.go, term.go, format.go, log.go, pane.go, snapshot.go) with full tests. Funnel Options gained Progress sink; emits scan_started/finished, stage_started/finished w/counts, agent_started/finished (finder=lens, verifier=candidate title), spend_tick (via recorder onRecord hook), budget_degraded/stopped. runRefuters signature changed to also return summed tokens (VerifyFinding updated). Daemon Deps gained Progress, threaded into per-cycle funnel via newFunnel; emits cycle_scheduled/started/finished, reverify, promote. CLI scan: TTY->PaneRenderer else LogRenderer, both + SnapshotSink, pane stopped before final summary. CLI daemon: SlogRenderer + SnapshotSink with daySpend getter. New 'bugbot status' command reads status.json (missing/stale-by-time/stale-by-dead-pid/fresh, exit 0 always, open findings count via store). All gates pass: build, vet (+tags), test -race, gofmt clean, golangci-lint 0 issues. DEVIATION: repro PromoteAll per-attempt emission SKIPPED to keep diff contained (Reproducer built by CLI before daemon progress sink exists; would need Options/signature churn). Promotion still visible via daemon's cycle-level KindPromote count event. Width detection is syscall-free ($COLUMNS fallback 80) per spec; TTY detection via os.ModeCharDevice.

title: Leads blackboard: cross-lens agent messaging
description: Finders trip over out-of-lens suspicions that currently evaporate (nil-safety finder notices dodgy locking). Add a leads table (poster role/lens, target lens, file/line, note, scan_run, status posted|consumed) and a post_lead tool for finders. The funnel injects pending leads into the matching lens's next task context (next chunk or next cycle in daemon mode) and marks them consumed. Deliberately asynchronous blackboard, NOT direct agent chat: persistent, deterministic, testable — and verify-stage refuters stay isolated by design since their independence is what kills false positives.
design: Migration 004_leads.sql: leads(id, scan_run_id, poster_lens, target_lens, file, line, note, status posted|consumed, created_at, consumed_at) with UNIQUE(target_lens,file,line) upsert as the near-identical dedup (re-post refreshes note, preserves created_at) — same key style as suppressions. Store CRUD: AddLead, PendingLeads(lens), ConsumeLeads(ids) following suppressions.go template. post_lead agent tool: FINDER-only (verifiers excluded by design — refuter independence is what kills FPs; documented in tool wiring); args {target_lens, file, line, note}; target_lens validated against BuiltinLenses names; posting to own lens allowed but pointless (documented). Injection: hypothesize claims pending leads per lens up-front (single-threaded, before parallel unit launch), marks consumed at claim time (a failed finder loses the lead — accepted trade-off, documented), injects a LEADS section into every chunk task for that lens. Stats: LeadsPosted/LeadsConsumed rendered in scan output + review footer. Cross-cycle: store is the carrier; funnel test seeds a lead via store then asserts next Sweep's concurrency-lens task contains it and marks consumed. Eval suite must stay precision 1.0.
acceptance_criteria: Migration adds leads table; post_lead tool for finder runners only (verifiers excluded by design, documented); funnel injects pending leads into matching lens prompts and marks consumed; dedup of near-identical leads; cross-cycle test: lens A posts a concurrency lead, next concurrency run receives it (daemon carries it across cycles); leads visible in scan stats; eval suite unchanged or better.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #12 (squash f385cdf), Sonnet implementer + skeptical review round. Decisions: migration 004 leads table with UNIQUE(target_lens,file,line) upsert as near-identical dedup; re-posting a consumed lead flips status back to posted (re-raised suspicion gets fresh attention), created_at preserved, consumed_at NULLed. post_lead tool finder-only with poster lens injected at construction (no impersonation); target validated against ALL builtin lenses so leads can target inactive lenses and stay pending until that lens runs; filepath.IsLocal blocks absolute+traversal paths; note capped 500 bytes. Hypothesize claims pending leads per lens single-threaded before parallel unit launch, consumes at claim time (failed finder loses the lead — documented trade-off), injects CROSS-LENS LEADS section into every chunk task for the lens; notes newline-flattened at render (review round caught the injection vector: an embedded newline could fabricate extra lead lines in a future finder's prompt). PendingLeads/ConsumeLeads errors degrade (note + continue), never abort. Refuters excluded at both verify.go and verify_finding.go with rationale comments, pinned by TestVerify_RefuterToolset_NoPostLead. Stats leads_posted/leads_consumed in scan + review footer. Cross-cycle proven by two-sweep funnel test driving the real agent tool-call loop. Acceptance fully met; eval gates precision 1.0. Live behavior (do real finders actually post useful leads?) unvalidated — fold into next live calibration run.

title: Debate verification: advocate/refuter/judge experiment
description: Experiment: replace independent-refuter majority voting with a structured debate — an advocate argues the candidate is real, a refuter argues it is not, a judge agent rules. Literature suggests debate improves judgment quality but it costs more tokens and trades away refuter independence, so it must EARN its place: implement as a pluggable verify strategy behind config and judge it with the eval harness (scripted + recorded corpora) against the majority-refutation baseline on precision, recall, and spend. Adopt, keep-as-option, or reject based on numbers; record the decision either way.
acceptance_criteria: Verify strategy is pluggable (majority|debate) via config with majority remaining default; debate implementation with bounded turns; eval comparison runnable as one command producing a side-by-side table (precision/recall/tokens per strategy); decision recorded on this bead with the numbers; if rejected, code stays behind config as an experiment, not deleted silently.

title: Memory: hash-anchored KV store + agent memory tools
description: Agents re-derive the same codebase facts every cycle — pure token waste in daemon mode. Add a memory table (key, value, provenance: role+scan_run, file anchors with content hashes, optional TTL/confidence) plus memory_get/memory_put/memory_search agent tools. Entries auto-invalidate when any anchored file's hash changes (reuses the finding re-verification machinery). Prompts must treat recalled entries as hints to verify, never ground truth — memory poisoning (one wrong fact propagating across cycles) is the failure mode; provenance + hash invalidation + hint framing are the mitigations.
acceptance_criteria: Migration adds memory table; three tools with schemas wired into finder/verifier runners; invalidation test: change an anchored file, entry no longer returned; TTL expiry test; provenance recorded and shown; prompt updates frame memory as unverified hints; poisoning posture documented in ARCHITECTURE.md; eval suite unchanged or better.

title: Refutation memory: machine-learned triage suppressions
description: Suppression memory only learns from human dismissals today. When refuters kill a candidate, persist the refutation (reason, anchored to the implicated file's content hash). Triage pre-kills matching future candidates for free while the anchor holds — and the entry expires the moment the file changes, so a stale refutation can never hide a bug introduced later. Extends the suppression concept from human-taught to machine-learned with automatic expiry.
acceptance_criteria: Refuter majority-kills write refutation entries keyed by candidate fingerprint pattern + file hash; triage consults them before verification and stats count the pre-kills separately; expiry test proving a changed file re-enables full verification; eval case: same bogus candidate found twice costs verifier tokens only once; a seeded real bug introduced after a refutation of similar shape is NOT suppressed (the critical safety test).

title: Git history tools + churn heat prioritization
description: Two wins from the existing git-CLI plumbing: (1) git_blame and git_log tools for agents — bug context, regression candidates, recent-change awareness; (2) a heat model (churn x recency, later x coverage-gap) that ranks files so sweep chunking spends finder budget where bugs statistically cluster instead of uniformly.
acceptance_criteria: git_blame/git_log tools with bounded output and fixture-repo tests; heat scoring in ingest or funnel orders sweep chunks (highest heat first) with unit tests; degradation when history is shallow; ordering observable in scan stats/logs; eval suite unchanged.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #27 (commit 78c41f5). git_blame (100-line clamp) + git_log (max 50) tools: 32KB bounds, 10s timeouts, -- separator (review probed flag injection + pathspec magic: repo-contained, documented), ERROR: degradation; wired to finders AND verifiers. ChurnHeat: one git log -n500 --since=180d call, score Σexp(-age/30d); Sweep sorts heat-desc/alphabetical-tiebreak (shallow history degrades automatically); Targeted untouched; KindHeatOrdered progress event + Stats fields. REVIEW MUST-FIXES: -c core.quotepath=off (non-ASCII paths were silently zero-heat) + 10s timeout on heat call. BONUS: real data race found+fixed in hypothesize.go (parallel finders appending post_lead into shared slice backing capacity; three-index append fix; reviewer-verified, revert-experiment-confirmed regression test). Follow-ups: bugbot-eg0 (git output injection surface), bugbot-cif (heat config knob).

title: Verifier sandbox-exec tool: evidence-based refutation
description: Only the repro stage executes code today; refuters argue rhetorically. Expose a gated sandbox execution tool to the verify stage so a refuter can empirically demonstrate a refutation (run the guard path, run the existing test suite) instead of asserting it. Converts verification from rhetoric to evidence — the core precision philosophy. Sandbox runs are expensive: gate by config flag and candidate severity, bound per-candidate executions, and record spend.
design: Funnel gains optional sandbox: Options.Sandbox (sandbox.Sandbox, nil = feature off) wired from cli/scan.go + daemon when config enables it. New config section verify: {sandbox_exec: bool (default false), sandbox_min_severity: string (default high), sandbox_max_execs: int per candidate (default 3)} + env overrides. New agent tool sandbox_exec exposed ONLY to refuter runners and ONLY when enabled + candidate severity >= min: args {cmd argv, files map[path]content like repro.Plan}, runs sandbox.Exec(Spec{RepoDir, Cmd, WriteFiles, Network:none}), returns exit/stdout/stderr/duration with tool-result output capped ~16KiB (sandbox's 1MiB cap is prompt-hostile). Per-candidate exec counter enforced inside the tool (refuters for one candidate run sequentially in its goroutine, so a plain counter per candidate works); exceeding returns a budget-exhausted tool error, never kills the run. Stats: SandboxExecs, SandboxExecMillis aggregated under existing result-mutex pattern, rendered in scan output + eval suite. Verifier prompt updated: prefer empirical demonstration over rhetoric when the tool is present. Eval: scripted client extended to optionally emit scripted tool calls (or funnel-level test with fake sandbox) demonstrating refute-via-execution; fake sandbox.Sandbox impl for tests. Network stays none always — tool never accepts a network arg.
acceptance_criteria: Sandbox exec tool exposed only to verifier-role runners behind a config flag with a severity gate; per-candidate execution count bounded; output capped; spend/duration recorded in scan stats; eval case demonstrating an empirical refutation flow; network stays none; full tree green.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #11 (squash 3e95208), implemented by a Sonnet agent + skeptical review round. Decisions: new config section verify{sandbox_exec off-by-default, sandbox_min_severity high, sandbox_max_execs 3} + BUGBOT_VERIFY_* env overrides; funnel Options.SandboxOpts{Sandbox, Enabled, MinSeverity, MaxExecs} with zero-value=off; sandbox_exec tool only for refuters on candidates at/above min severity (unknown severities rank 4 = conservatively excluded); args {cmd, files} mirror repro.Plan; network hardcoded none, additionalProperties:false blocks smuggling image/timeout; non-zero exit = normal result; per-candidate atomic exec counter (refuters sequential per candidate) returns tool error on exhaustion; 16KiB per-stream prompt cap over the backend 1MiB cap; infra errors -> tool errors (refuter falls back to rhetoric). Stats SandboxExecs/SandboxExecMillis aggregated via atomics folded under result mutex; rendered in scan + PR-review footer. Refuter prompt: prefer empirical demonstration, execution confirming bug = do NOT refute (inversion guarded). Daemon VerifyFinding deliberately excluded (re-verify runs per finding per code change — container spend) — documented + pinned by TestVerifyFinding_NoSandboxTool. Review round must-fixes applied: explicit warning when enabled-but-no-runtime (scan/review/daemon), atomic test fakes. Eval-case acceptance satisfied at funnel level: TestSweep_SandboxExec_StatsAggregated drives a scripted tool-call client through the real agent runner loop with a fake sandbox; eval ScriptedClient left untouched (extending it for tool calls was disproportionate). Live podman validation not yet done — fold into next live calibration run.

title: Code-nav tools: LSP-backed find_definition/find_references/find_implementations (multi-language)
description: Code navigation tools for agents, LSP-first (general, not Go-only — user direction 2026-06-09, supersedes the go/packages v1 bugbot-24n). Refuters currently argue from grep output, which is noisy and misses guards. Add find_definition / find_references (callers) / find_implementations tools to the agent harness backed by a hand-rolled LSP client (JSON-RPC 2.0 over stdio, Content-Length framing, ~5 methods — no official Go client lib; x/tools client is internal/, community libs stale). The real work is server lifecycle: per-language spawn (gopls, typescript-language-server, pyright, clangd), initialize handshake + capability negotiation, crash restart, clean shutdown, discovery by file extension, graceful degradation when servers are missing. Simplifiers: agents are read-only (no didChange sync) and tool errors already round-trip to the model. Wire into funnel readOnlyTools(); update refuter prompt to prefer find_references when checking whether callers already guard a claimed bug.
acceptance_criteria: LSP client speaks JSON-RPC 2.0 over stdio with correct Content-Length framing and UTF-16 position conversion (non-ASCII test case required). Tools registered with JSON schemas and wired into finder/verifier tool sets. Server lifecycle managed: spawn, initialize handshake, bounded warm-up wait (never hang; degrade to a clear 'unavailable' tool error), crash restart, clean shutdown. Language server discovery by extension with graceful degradation message when binary missing. Unit tests with a fake/scripted server for protocol + lifecycle; integration tests gated on real server availability (gopls; skip cleanly when absent) like sandbox tests. Refuter prompt updated. go build/vet/test and gofmt clean; golangci-lint clean.
notes: PR #5 open, branch lsp-code-nav (commit fdd6167). Close when merged. Gotcha: gopls zero-references on interface methods not called via interface. Follow-up beads: repro tool set lacks code-nav; daemon-lifetime LSP manager for warm indexes; bugbot-0ze tree-sitter fallback.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #5 (merged to main 9edf9a6). LSP-first per user direction, superseding go/packages v1. Hand-rolled 5-method JSON-RPC client (internal/lsp), lifecycle: lazy per-language spawn, capability gating, crash-restart-once-then-disable, bounded 60s queries, polite shutdown with kill escalation. Tools take {file,line,symbol} — tool resolves column itself. UTF-16 verified vs live gopls (emoji/CJK) and clangd. Post-review hardening: symlink containment on result paths, 16KB header bound, -race concurrency tests. Known gotcha: gopls returns zero references for interface methods never called through the interface. Follow-ups: bugbot-0ze (tree-sitter fallback), bugbot-yc8 (repro tools), bugbot-cy8 (daemon-lifetime manager), bugbot-ya3 (conn robustness lows).

comments.author: Dustin
comments.text: Implementation complete on PR #5 (https://github.com/dpoage/bugbot/pull/5). All gates green locally (build/vet/test/gofmt, CI-exact golangci-lint 0 issues, -race clean on lsp+agent, integration vs real gopls AND clangd incl. UTF-16 emoji/CJK case). Merges cleanly with PR #4 in either order (merge-tree verified, 0 conflict markers). Design: new internal/lsp package (jsonrpc framing / conn with server-request answering / server lifecycle / manager with crash-restart-then-disable); tools take {file,line,symbol} and resolve the column themselves so the model never guesses columns. Gotcha for future sessions: gopls returns ZERO references for an interface method never called through the interface type — 'no references' output is not proof of no callers. Independent code review in flight; close bead when PR merged.

comments.author: Dustin
comments.text: Code review of PR #5 complete. Findings fixed in fb1189c: H1 symlink content-leak on result paths (relPath now mirrors fsRoot.resolve symlink containment), M3 unbounded header read loop (16KB budget), L6 misleading indexing-timeout message on parent deadlines, M2+L7 missing concurrency/-race coverage (4 new tests: concurrent conn calls w/ shuffled responses, transport death racing senders, concurrent lazy spawn, queries across crash-restart; FAKE_LSP_STALL_ON knob replaces the nanosecond-timeout hack). L4+L5 (read-loop reply wedge, stderr-tail vs EOF race) deferred to bugbot-ya3. Reviewer confirmed clean: UTF-16 conversion, Close() wiring on all exit paths, dead-channel/pending-map race safety, frame parsing edge cases, symbol resolution, input-path sandboxing. All gates re-run green after fixes.

title: Code-nav tools v1: find_definition/find_callers via go/packages (Go)
description: Refuters currently argue from grep output, which is noisy and misses guards. Add call-graph navigation tools to the agent harness: find_definition, find_callers (and find_implementations for interfaces), with language-agnostic tool names/schemas and a Go backend via golang.org/x/tools/go/packages (new dep, acceptable). Wire into finder and verifier runners when the target repo is Go. Update the refuter prompt to prefer find_callers when checking whether callers already guard a claimed bug.
acceptance_criteria: Tools registered for Go repos with JSON schemas; table-driven tests on a fixture package covering functions, methods, and interface implementations; graceful degradation message for non-Go targets; refuter prompt updated; eval suite precision unchanged or better; full tree green.

title: Implement internal/repro: Tier-1 reproduce stage
description: Build the Reproduce stage per bugbot-v2f.8. Reproducer agent (read-only tools) generates a minimal failing test for a T2 finding, executes it in the sandbox (network none), interprets exit codes, guards against compile-error false reproductions, writes artifacts (test files + run.sh + README.md) on success, promotes finding to T1 with repro_path. PromoteAll runs bounded-parallel over T2 findings. Wire a --repro flag into internal/cli/scan.go.
acceptance_criteria: internal/repro package with Reproducer.New, Attempt, PromoteAll, Summary; go vet ./internal/repro/... ./internal/cli/...; go test -race ./internal/repro/... green; success/zero-exit-revision/compile-guard/exhaustion unit tests with fake llm.Client + sandbox.NewMock + real store in t.TempDir; optional integration test behind build tag. gofmt clean.
close_reason: Implemented internal/repro: Reproducer{New,Attempt,PromoteAll,Summary,Plan,Attempt,FindingOutcome,Options}. Reproducer agent uses read-only tools (read_file/list_dir/grep) + RunJSON with a plan schema {files,cmd,expect}; system prompt demands a minimal assertion-bearing failing test that fails on buggy code and passes when fixed. Execution via sandbox Spec network=none. interpret() promotion rules: exit0=>not demonstrated, timeout=>not, non-zero with build-failure markers=>not (false-repro guard), else demonstrated. Non-demonstrating attempts feed category-specific feedback back for revision up to MaxAttempts. On success writes ArtifactDir/<id>/ bundle (repro files + run.sh + README.md) and PromoteAll updates store to T1 + repro_path via a SQL-free wrapper over GetFindingByFingerprint+UpsertFinding (no new store method needed). Failure leaves finding at T2 and removes partial artifacts. PromoteAll bounded by MaxParallel (default 2). Wired --repro into cli/scan.go with sandbox.Detect graceful skip. Tests: success, zero-exit->revision->success, compile-error guard, exhaustion (all real store in t.TempDir, sandbox.NewMock, scripted fake client) + integration test behind build tag 'integration' with real podman/golang image and hand-written plan. go vet + go test -race green, gofmt clean.

title: Reporting layer: report pkg (md/SARIF emitters, sinks) + report CLI
description: Build internal/report (Markdown + SARIF 2.1.0 emitters over []store.Finding+metadata, Sink interface with FSSink/StdoutSink, SinksFromConfig registry) and rewrite internal/cli/report.go (list/show/dismiss/emit). Package must stay independent of funnel to avoid import cycles for daemon reuse.
acceptance_criteria: go vet + go test -race ./internal/report/... green; gofmt clean on new files; SARIF unmarshals with required non-empty fields and severity->level mapping; markdown golden test deterministic; dismiss writes suppression (IsSuppressed true) and list --status dismissed shows it; ID-prefix resolution handles exact/prefix/ambiguous.
close_reason: Built internal/report (report.go core/sort, markdown.go, sarif.go hand-rolled 2.1.0 structs, sink.go FSSink+StdoutSink+SinksFromConfig, query.go CollectOpen+ResolveID) and rewrote internal/cli/report.go with list/show/dismiss/emit. Dismiss uses store.AddSuppression (atomic suppress+flip) per spec. SinksFromConfig accepts fs/stdout and treats legacy markdown/sarif as FSSink aliases (config default ships 'markdown') collapsing to one FSSink. Package is funnel-independent (operates on store.Finding + small findingLister iface) so daemon/scan can reuse via report.CollectOpen. Tests: markdown golden, SARIF shape+severity mapping+repo-relative URIs, FSSink latest copies, full dismiss flow vs real store, ID-prefix resolution (exact/prefix/ambiguous), CLI end-to-end. go vet + go test -race + gofmt all green on owned packages; go build ./... succeeds.

title: Daemon mode: scheduler, budgets, backoff, re-verification
description: bugbot daemon: scheduler interleaving the slow whole-repo sweep with commit/PR-triggered targeted investigations (blast-radius scoped); token budgets per cycle and per day with graceful degradation (fewer lenses, defer deep verification); idle backoff when repo unchanged; re-verify open findings whose implicated code changed and auto-close fixed ones.
acceptance_criteria: Daemon detects a new commit in a fixture repo and runs a targeted investigation; budget exhaustion halts LLM spend with a clear log line; a fixed seeded bug is auto-closed on the next cycle; idle repo produces near-zero spend.
close_reason: Daemon built and verified (6 tests, -race, injectable clock so no wall-time sleeps: commit->targeted-scan flow, idle backoff growth, day-budget skip with zero funnel calls, auto-close of fixed findings, graceful shutdown). Decisions: re-verify seam = new additive funnel.VerifyFinding reusing runRefuters/majorityRefuted at default count (majority-refuted = auto-close-as-fixed signal); last-seen commit in file_state sentinel row @daemon/last-seen — slash prefix cannot collide with repo paths, avoids a migration; MarkFixed not dismiss for resolved findings so reintroduced bugs can reopen; spend-read errors fail open (per-cycle budget still bounds); hash-unchanged findings never touched; survivors re-anchored to new hash to avoid per-cycle re-verification.

title: Evaluation harness: seeded-bug benchmarks, precision/recall
description: Ground truth for tuning precision: mutation/bug-injection benchmark over healthy fixture repos measuring detection rate AND false-positive rate on unmutated code; regression corpus of real confirmed bugs; runnable in CI via recorded LLM transcripts (from harness transcript capture) so results are cheap and comparable.
acceptance_criteria: Single command produces precision/recall numbers per lens and per stage; offline mode runs from recorded fixtures with no API calls; results comparable across runs for prompt/pipeline regression detection.
close_reason: Eval harness built and verified (28 tests, -race; suite runs at precision 1.000, tp=5 fp=0 fn=0). THE command: go test ./internal/eval/ -run TestBenchmarkSuite -v. Decisions: recorded replay is two-level (role, run-sequence)->session because funnel shares one client per role across runners; MaxParallel=1 default in eval since parallelism breaks sequence-matched replay; bug Kind is a label not a match gate (file+line matching only) so cross-lens detection still counts; vacuous-perfect convention avoids NaN on clean cases; suppressed case asserted to die in triage not verification. Real-model transcript corpus is future work; mechanism proven with synthetic recordings. RunSuite ready for a future bugbot eval command.

title: Reporting: markdown + SARIF emitters, sinks, report CLI, suppression
description: Report generation and management: markdown and SARIF emitters (reasoning chain, tier, repro artifact link); pluggable sink interface (filesystem, stdout now; GitHub issues/webhook later); bugbot report list/show/dismiss CLI where dismiss writes suppression memory.
acceptance_criteria: Emitted SARIF validates against the SARIF 2.1 schema; dismiss causes the finding to be suppressed in subsequent scans; markdown report contains file:line, tier, reasoning chain, and repro link when present.
close_reason: Reporting built and verified (golden markdown, SARIF shape tests, FSSink + latest copies, dismiss->IsSuppressed flow against real store, ID prefix resolution). Decisions: dismiss uses AddSuppression (suppression row + status flip in one txn, the path UpsertFinding honors forever) not UpdateStatus; package store-decoupled via 1-method findingLister so daemon reuses CollectOpen cycle-free; SARIF validation by-construction with schema validation deferred to CI; legacy markdown/sarif sink names aliased to fs, config default/template updated to fs by orchestrator.

title: Reproduce stage: sandboxed failing-test generation (Tier 1)
description: For verified findings, a reproducer agent writes a minimal failing test or repro script and executes it in the sandbox. Demonstrated failure promotes the finding to Tier 1 with the artifact attached; failed repro demotes to T2 but does not delete.
acceptance_criteria: Seeded-bug fixture yields at least one T1 finding with a runnable repro artifact; failed repro attempts demote rather than drop; artifacts stored and linked from reports.
close_reason: Repro stage built and verified (unit suite race-clean with scripted client + sandbox mock; integration test against real podman + golang:1.26-alpine behind integration tag, offline-capable fixture). Decisions: promotion ruleset is conservative — non-zero exit with build/dep markers treated as failed attempt not demonstration (false-repro guard errs toward demotion); failed repro demotes nothing (T2 stays); Attempt is store-free, PromoteAll owns persistence and surfaces persist-failures as errors; store untouched — promotion via GetFindingByFingerprint + UpsertFinding re-read to avoid clobbering; default parallelism 2 due to workspace copy cost.

title: Repro as a pipeline operator: wire verified findings into the repro backlog continuously, under the global agent cap
description: Completes Dustin's full-dataflow direction (2026-06-11): with bugbot-280 streaming candidates through finder->triage->verify, the repro stage joins the same dataflow. Key insight: repro is ALREADY the architectural template — internal/daemon/backlog.go is a durable queue over the findings store with oldest-first rotation (failed attempts rotate to the back) and idempotent attempts; publish is fingerprint-idempotent downstream. What changes: (1) verified T2 findings persisted mid-run become immediately eligible for repro (today the scan --repro flag runs repro as a post-stage and the daemon drains on idle) — the repro consumer runs continuously as a background-class operator; (2) repro agents draw from bugbot-280's GLOBAL agent cap as the lowest-priority role class (verify > finder > repro), since repro is sandbox-heavy, latency-tolerant, and its rotation loses nothing by waiting — an idle-slots-only policy is acceptable v1; (3) interruption semantics come free from the existing backlog durability. Explicitly out of scope: changing repro attempt logic, sandbox mechanics, or promotion rules — this is call topology + scheduling only. Blocked by bugbot-280's streaming phases (the global cap must exist first).
acceptance_criteria: (1) During a long scan, a finding verified early gets a repro attempt before the finder stage completes (integration test with scripted clients + fake sandbox asserting interleaved roles in agent_units). (2) Repro agents never displace a waiting verifier or finder slot under the global cap (scheduling test). (3) Kill mid-run: repro attempts made are recorded, backlog rotation state intact, nothing double-attempted on resume. (4) Daemon idle-drain behavior unchanged when no scan is running. (5) Gates clean.

title: Advisory single-scan lock: warn or refuse when a second scan targets the same state db
description: Motivated by a real incident (2026-06-11 dogfood): two identical 'bugbot scan' processes ran concurrently against the same config/state db, silently double-spending API tokens and contending for rate limits (~10 min before noticed via ps + agent_units scan_run_id counts). Process-level parallelism is the cousin of bugbot-280's in-process global agent cap, out of scope there. Fix: an advisory lock keyed on the state db — e.g. a 'running scan' row in scan_runs (started, not finished, recent heartbeat) checked at scan start; on conflict, refuse with a clear message naming the running scan_run_id/pid and a --force override (stale-lock tolerant: a scan_run with no heartbeat for N minutes is considered dead — the bugbot-280 phase-1 interrupted-finalization work makes stale rows rarer). Ecosystem-general, store-level, no platform file-locking needed.
acceptance_criteria: (1) Second concurrent scan against the same store refuses by default, names the live run, and --force overrides. (2) Stale (heartbeat-expired or unfinalized-old) locks do not block. (3) Daemon's own poll/sweep cycles are unaffected. (4) Test covers refuse, force, and stale-lock paths. (5) Gates clean.

title: Record reproducer units in agent_units (mi5.10 acceptance gap)
description: bugbot-mi5.10 shipped finder and verifier unit rows (PR #48) but the reproducer stage records nothing — acceptance criterion 1 asked for verifier/reproducer symmetry, the Sonnet implementer silently omitted it, and the review round's named seams did not cover the repro stage so it survived to merge (closed with the deviation documented). Extend internal/funnel/observability.go's recordVerifierUnit pattern to the repro stage: one row per repro agent run with role='reproducer', the finding's lens/file, status from the repro outcome classification mapped to short snake_case, tokens from the agent Outcome, detail = outcome summary (no model text). Same best-effort discipline (never abort a scan) and outside-the-mutex insert if the repro stage has shared state.
acceptance_criteria: (1) One agent_units row per reproducer agent run with role='reproducer' and a status vocabulary documented in the migration or agentunits.go comment. (2) Funnel/repro test drives a repro attempt via fakes and asserts the row (status, nonzero tokens, lens/file). (3) report units renders reproducer rows without breaking the finder-only coverage fraction. (4) Gates clean.

title: Agent activity feed: show what each in-flight agent is up to (tool-derived + optional hypothesis notes)
description: Status view / future watch TUI (bugbot-7td) shows only role+lens+elapsed per in-flight agent. Add a per-turn 'current activity' string so the user can see what each agent is actually doing. TIER 1 (mechanical): runner emits KindAgentActivity per turn derived from tool calls ('reading file X', 'grepping Y') — zero token cost, cannot misreport. TIER 2 (flag-gated): a lightweight status_note tool lets the agent self-report its current hypothesis ('suspecting double-close in retry loop'). Tier 2 doubles as a data feed for bugbot-mi5.10's distribution questions: timestamped hypothesis notes measure WHEN/WHERE hypotheses form (turn number, launch order), which run-level stats aggregate away. Risk noted: finder prompt forbids speculation; hypothesis broadcasting could bias agents toward performing hypotheses — frame as non-binding scratchpad note, keep behind a flag.
design: Seam: agents are in-process goroutines (internal/agent/runner.go), so the runner's turn loop can emit progress events directly — no transcript tailing needed. Touchpoints: events.go (new KindAgentActivity), snapshot.go (extend AgentStatus + apply()), runner.go (emit per turn), pane.go/status.go (render). Flows to bugbot-7td watch TUI for free via status.json. Rejected alternative: tailing transcript JSONL files — pointless given same-process access; adds I/O latency and parsing noise.
acceptance_criteria: (1) AgentStatus in internal/progress/snapshot.go carries an activity string + last-update time; status.json includes it. (2) Runner emits a KindAgentActivity event per turn derived from tool calls with no extra LLM cost. (3) Pane/log renderers and bugbot status display the activity line per in-flight agent. (4) Hypothesis self-report tool exists behind a flag, off by default; when on, notes appear as activity and land in the transcript. (5) Activity strings are truncated/sanitized for single-line terminal rendering. (6) No behavior change to finder output schema or verdicts.
notes: CORRECTION to the risk framing in description (2026-06-11): the finder prompt does NOT forbid speculation per se — it forbids REPORTING speculation as a finding (prompt.go:27-38: 'Report ONLY concrete, confirmed bugs', 'do NOT pad with weak or speculative entries', low-confidence entries dropped). Internal hypothesizing is the design (stage is named hypothesize; funnel is hypothesize→refute). Precedent: cross-lens LEADS (prompt.go:174) already inject other agents' suspicions ('they may be wrong') into finder tasks — speculation as a shared artifact is sanctioned. The actual Tier-2 risk is channel confusion: the status_note prompt wording must keep the candidates-array bar unchanged so notes neither become a pressure valve that relaxes reporting discipline nor a back door for unconfirmed findings. Flag-gating stays as the containment.

title: Cross-model finder ensembles per lens
description: Different model families have measurably non-overlapping bug-finding distributions. The llm package already maps roles to provider+model; extend config so a lens (or the finder role generally) can fan out to 2+ models, with triage's dedup fingerprints absorbing cross-model duplicates. Natural two-tier variant: a cheap model runs wide sweeps posting leads; the expensive model runs deep dives on lead-rich areas. Evaluate added unique recall per dollar before defaulting it on — this is the most speculative item in the epic and should be driven by eval data from the recorded corpus.
acceptance_criteria: (1) Config supports multiple finder models per lens or role; default remains single-model. (2) Dedup verified to collapse cross-model duplicate candidates. (3) Eval report quantifies unique-finding contribution and token cost per added model. (4) Budget accounting attributes spend per model.

title: Coverage-gap targeting + reproduce-first mode (findings born T1)
description: Two coupled pieces. (a) Targeting: compute per-file test-coverage presence per ecosystem and intersect with churn heat — high-churn x uncovered is the strongest bug prior available without running anything. Use it to rank sweep targets. (b) Reproduce-first: for a small budget-capped set of top-ranked targets, skip hypothesize entirely — an agent reads doc comments / declared contracts and writes property or round-trip tests, run in the sandbox via the per-ecosystem SetupCmds machinery (bugbot-93z.8/93z.11). A failing test IS the finding, born at T1 with the repro attached. Expensive per target, so it only runs on the coverage-gap-ranked shortlist. Coverage detection can start coarse (does any test file reference this module) before integrating real coverage tooling per ecosystem.
acceptance_criteria: (1) Sweep targeting incorporates churn x uncovered ranking with stats showing the intersection size. (2) Reproduce-first runs only on a configurable top-N shortlist within budget. (3) A failing generated test produces a T1 finding with the test attached as repro evidence; a passing suite produces nothing (no speculative candidates). (4) Generated tests never leak outside the sandbox workspace.

title: doctor: warn when sandbox image lacks the detected language's toolchain (C++ cmake/g++, Python pip)
description: The default sandbox image (docker.io/library/debian:stable-slim) has no C++ toolchain (cmake/ninja/g++) and no pip — so C++ repro (configure+build, per bugbot-93z.5's guidance) and Python dep installs (bugbot-93z.11 SetupCmds) fail at runtime on the default image, with errors that surface deep in the repro stage. Deployments must set sandbox.image appropriately (e.g. gcc:13 or python:3-slim), but nothing tells the operator upfront. Add a doctor informational/warn check: when dominant languages imply toolchain needs (cpp -> cmake or make + a compiler; python -> pip), probe the configured image for those binaries (single '<runtime> run --rm <image> sh -c "command -v ..."' with the existing 5s-probe-timeout pattern, skipped when the image is not present locally) and WARN naming the missing tools and suggesting an image. Never hard-fail (image may be intentionally minimal with deps handled elsewhere). Also add commented sandbox.image guidance per language family in config/template.go. Cross-ref: 93z.5 close reason recorded the C++ image/timeout concern; 93z.11 design records the pip gap.
acceptance_criteria: On a config pointing at debian:stable-slim with a dominant C++ repo, doctor WARNs naming cmake/compiler absence and suggests an image; Python repo likewise for pip; image-not-local skips the probe with an INFO; probe respects timeout; never affects exit code; unit tests with injected runCommand fakes; template.go gains per-language image guidance comments.

title: doctor: lighter language detection than full content-sniffing snapshot
description: bugbot doctor's languages check builds a full ingest.Snapshot — reading and binary-sniffing every tracked file — purely to print a one-line INFO list of dominant languages (internal/cli/doctor.go checkRepo). On large repos that is slow and heavy for an informational diagnostic, and it is why the repo-facts group needs a 30s timeout. Flagged in the adversarial review of PR bugbot-64p (finding 4). Consider an extension-only scan over git ls-files (no content read), or a Snapshot fast-path that skips binary sniffing. Related nit from the same review: TestDoctor_SecretsNeverInOutput only exercises the happy path; extend it to a config-error path while in here.
acceptance_criteria: doctor's languages check no longer reads file contents (verify: doctor on a large repo completes the languages line in well under the 30s repo-facts timeout; no os.ReadFile of tracked files on that path). DominantLanguages results match the snapshot-based ones on this repo. Secrets test covers at least one error path.

title: Cross-language dep-strategy verification matrix + docs (README strategy table per ecosystem)
description: Capstone for the dep-strategy children (93z.8-93z.11): the README currently documents the vendored/off/host/fetch strategies for Go only. Extend the strategy table to a per-ecosystem matrix (Go/Rust/JS/Python: what 'vendored' means there, host-cache path, prefetch command, offline-enforcement env, whether an in-sandbox setup step runs), document the per-ecosystem security boundaries (npm --ignore-scripts in prefetch; never mounting ~/.cargo credentials; SELinux Shared-mount semantics), and add a single integration-tagged matrix test that exercises one external-dep fixture per ecosystem under network-none (skipping per-language when the toolchain image is unavailable). Also satisfies the epic acceptance line 'each language layer table documents its coverage tier' for the deps layer.
acceptance_criteria: README matrix covers Go/Rust/JS/Python with strategy semantics + security notes per ecosystem; integration matrix test exists with per-language skips; deps.go header comment lists the ecosystem table's coverage tiers; gates green.

title: Config knob for sweep heat ordering (currently Options-only, silently always-on)
description: Review finding on bugbot-sro: funnel Options.DisableHeatOrdering exists but is wired from no config field or env var — heat ordering is always-on for every user and un-disableable without code edits. It degrades to alphabetical when git history is unavailable, so this is acceptable short-term. Add a config home (likely a new scan: section or budgets-adjacent field) + env override + wiring in scan.go/daemon.go/review.go.
acceptance_criteria: Config field + env override toggles heat ordering; default on; wired at all three funnel-construction sites; config tests follow existing patterns.

title: Prompt-injection surface: git_log/git_blame return attacker-adjacent commit metadata verbatim to agents
description: Review finding on bugbot-sro: git_log returns author+subject and git_blame returns author+line content verbatim to finder/verifier models. In public repos commit messages are attacker-controllable, so a malicious commit subject could carry prompt-injection text. Likely acceptable today (models already read attacker-adjacent file content) but the surface is wider and undocumented. Evaluate sanitization (flatten whitespace like leads notes do in funnel finderTask), an explicit untrusted-content framing line in tool output, or accept-and-document.
acceptance_criteria: Decision recorded: either sanitization/framing implemented for git tool output (with tests) or an explicit accept-risk rationale documented in the tool source; consistent with how leads notes are sanitized.

title: Detection batch for minor languages: Zig, Gleam, Elixir, Scala, Dart, Lua, Obj-C
description: These languages resolve to LangOther today, making them invisible to personas and language stats. Add extension entries to extLang plus displayName: Zig (.zig), Gleam (.gleam), Elixir (.ex/.exs), Scala (.scala/.sc), Dart (.dart), Lua (.lua), Objective-C (.m/.mm — note .m is a real omission given .h already maps to C; beware MATLAB also uses .m, accept the misclassification or sniff content). Where the ecosystem has a single build marker and uniform test command, optionally add build detection + repro guidance in the same pass: build.zig / zig build test, gleam.toml / gleam test, mix.exs / mix test. Scala/Dart/Lua get detection only for now.
acceptance_criteria: Each listed extension classifies to its language in DetectLanguage and renders in personas via displayName. DominantLanguages counts them. Zig/Gleam/Elixir build markers detected if included in this pass (otherwise file follow-ups). gofmt/vet/tests clean.

title: Polish: fix_patch diff headers leak temp-dir paths
description: Live validation (bugbot-7ib) showed computeDiff's 'git diff --no-index' output uses the patched-copy temp path in headers (e.g. 'b/tmp/bugbot-patch-diff-NNN/mathx.go') instead of a clean b/mathx.go. Cosmetic but visible in every report/issue body carrying a fix witness.
acceptance_criteria: fix_patch headers read a/<path> b/<path> with no temp components; unit test on header shape; existing patch tests green.

title: bugbot watch: live TUI dashboard over status.json + store
description: Follow-up to bugbot-awl (pane of glass). The snapshot sink already rate-limits status.json to ~1s for exactly this: a live-refreshing terminal dashboard (bugbot watch) rendering activity, agents in flight, spend ticker, and findings/leads as they land. Build after the static status layout has proven what belongs on screen.
acceptance_criteria: bugbot watch live-refreshes without flicker; degrades to static snapshot when no activity; quits cleanly; no LLM/network use; reuses the status world-state renderers.

title: Regression hunter: findings introduced within a commit range
description: Answer 'what got worse since X' instead of 'what is wrong'. 'bugbot regress --from A --to B' (and a daemon weekly report): restrict scanning to the blast radius of A..B (the --since machinery generalized to a range), then attribute each finding as INTRODUCED (absent at A, present at B — re-verify the anchor against A's content) vs PRE-EXISTING. Daemon emits a per-cycle/per-week 'new since last green' digest. Synergy: the git history tools + churn heat prioritization planned in bugbot-sro provide the plumbing (commit-range diffing, blame); build on it.
acceptance_criteria: regress command scans only the A..B blast radius and labels findings introduced vs pre-existing with the evidence (anchor check at A); daemon digest lists introduced-this-period findings; works when A's tree lacks the file (treated as introduced); fixture test with a bug added in a known commit; tree green + lint clean.

title: Doc-contract drift lens: verify code honors its documented contracts
description: Bugs linters cannot see by construction: doc comments state contracts ('caller must hold mu', 'returns nil when X', 'safe for concurrent use', 'must be closed by caller') that the implementation or callers silently violate. New built-in lens: finder extracts explicit contract claims from doc comments in the chunk, then uses the code-nav tools (find_references — now cheap) to check callers/implementation against each claim. High-precision target: only EXPLICIT normative statements (must/never/always/safe-for), not vibes. The committed M3 corpus and this repo's own internal/lsp (rich doc contracts) are good first targets.
acceptance_criteria: Lens ships as a built-in; finder prompt extracts only explicit normative claims and verifies via code-nav with the claim quoted in the candidate; fixture eval case with a seeded doc-contract violation (e.g. 'must hold mu' violated) is found and survives refutation; no measurable precision regression on the recorded corpus replay; tree green + lint clean.

title: Repo-specific lens generation: meta-agent writes custom lenses
description: Generic lenses find generic bugs; generated lenses find THIS repo's bugs. A meta-agent runs once per repo (and on significant change): reads README/CLAUDE.md/doc comments/build files, identifies frameworks, conventions, and invariants ('shells out to podman — audit arg construction'; 'store dedups by fingerprint — check every insert computes it'), and emits custom lens definitions (name, finder prompt, scope hints) persisted in the store and versioned. Generated lenses run alongside built-ins, clearly labeled in findings. Human can list/disable them ('bugbot lens list/disable'). Guardrails: cap count, require the meta-agent to cite the evidence (file:line) each lens is derived from, and never let a generated lens widen scan scope beyond config.
acceptance_criteria: Meta-agent produces N<=cap lenses each citing derivation evidence; persisted + versioned in store; sweep runs them alongside built-ins with provenance labels in report; lens list/disable works; regeneration is idempotent on an unchanged repo; tree green + lint clean.

title: Per-lens precision calibration: auto-tune verification spend
description: Track each lens's historical precision (from eval corpus replays + human verdicts: dismissals vs confirmed/published findings) in the store, and let the funnel auto-tune refuter count per lens: noisy lenses get more adversarial verification, consistently-precise lenses get the minimum. Keep it transparent: scan summary shows effective refuter counts and why. Bounded: never below 1 or above configured max; cold-start (no history) uses the configured default. Depends on dismissal data existing (false-positive memory bead).
acceptance_criteria: Store accumulates per-lens outcome counts; funnel derives refuter count per lens within configured bounds; summary/report show the derived counts with the precision stats behind them; deterministic given fixed history (unit test); cold-start behaves exactly as today; tree green + lint clean.

title: SARIF export for GitHub code scanning and tool interop
description: Findings -> SARIF 2.1.0 so results land in GitHub's Security/code-scanning tab and interoperate with other analysis tooling. 'bugbot export --format sarif' (and a scan flag) mapping: lens -> rule, tier -> level (T1/T2 -> error/warning, T3 -> note), fingerprint -> partialFingerprints, reasoning -> message, file:line -> physicalLocation. Keep it dependency-light (hand-rolled struct marshaling; the schema subset we emit is small).
acceptance_criteria: Exported SARIF validates against the 2.1.0 schema (testdata golden file + a validator in tests if feasible); uploadable via gh code-scanning upload on a fixture run; stable fingerprints across runs; tree green + lint clean.

title: LSP conn robustness: async server-request replies; prefer stderr-tail crash errors over bare EOF
description: Two low-severity findings from the bugbot-tmb code review (PR #5), deferred. (1) conn.readLoop answers server->client requests synchronously under wmu (conn.go respondToServer): a misbehaving server that stops reading our stdin while still sending requests blocks the write, stops the read loop draining, and wedges the connection until process exit — callers stay bounded by the 60s query timeout but the read goroutine leaks until process death. Fix: route replies through a buffered channel drained by a dedicated writer goroutine, or make reply writes best-effort/non-blocking. (2) markDead is once-guarded and races between readLoop (bare EOF) and the cmd.Wait goroutine (which carries the stderr tail explaining the crash); EOF often wins so the user-facing error drops the diagnostic stderr. Fix: have the EOF path defer briefly to the process-exit error, or prefer a non-EOF deadErr.
acceptance_criteria: Reply write-back cannot stall the read loop (test: server sends requests but never reads stdin; conn keeps draining and Close stays bounded); crash errors include the stderr tail deterministically (test: crashing fake server with stderr output, assert tail present in the query error); -race clean; tree green + lint clean.

title: Daemon-lifetime LSP server manager: keep language-server indexes warm across cycles
description: bugbot-tmb (PR #5) spawns and shuts down language servers per scan/cycle via Funnel.Close(). gopls indexing takes seconds-to-minutes on medium repos, so per-cycle spawn re-pays warm-up every cycle. Lift the lsp.Manager to daemon lifetime: construct once at daemon boot, share across cycles, pre-warm at boot, shut down on daemon exit. Was in the original v2 scoping ('daemon mode is the natural fit: start servers at boot, amortize indexing warm-up across cycles').
acceptance_criteria: Daemon constructs one lsp.Manager at boot and reuses it across cycles (verify gopls PID stable across two cycles in an integration test or log assertion); clean shutdown on daemon exit incl. signal paths; one-shot scan behavior unchanged (per-scan lifecycle); crash-restart semantics still bounded; tree green + lint clean.

title: Give repro agents the code-nav tools
description: internal/repro builds its own readOnlyTools(dir) (repro.go:258) and was an intentional scope cut in bugbot-tmb (PR #5): repro agents get read/list/grep but not find_definition/find_references/find_implementations. Add a CodeNav instance (with Close hook) to the repro tool set so reproduction agents can navigate precisely too.
acceptance_criteria: Repro agent tool set includes the three code-nav tools backed by the shared lsp.Manager pattern; CodeNav closed on all repro exit paths; existing repro tests pass; tree green + lint clean.

value: Sonnet vs Opus implementer agents (n=2 each, PRs 9-12, 2026-06-10): Sonnet + detailed spec + adversarial review ships at Opus-equivalent quality for well-specified features, ~3-5x cheaper. Sonnet executed everything explicitly spec'd correctly (incl. subtle SQL upsert semantics, atomics, severity-rank inequality); its misses were one-step-beyond-the-spec defensive edges (warn-when-config-enabled-but-degraded; sanitizing model-authored text crossing a prompt boundary) and process discipline (left commit on worktree branch with feature branch as dangling pointer; pushed unasked; thin report, skipped pasted gates). Mitigations that worked: (1) orchestrator does investigation+design FIRST and writes the seams/edge-cases/file-refs into the prompt; (2) prompt hardening: 'git checkout -b BRANCH first', 'paste gate output', 'do not push'; (3) tell the code-reviewer the implementer was a smaller model -> review extra-skeptically, and NAME the highest-risk seam explicitly (the injection finding came from naming it); (4) orchestrator re-runs all gates incl -race. Keep Opus (or design inline) for ambiguity-heavy work: bugbot-dtq memory-poisoning design is the canonical example. Review round caught real must-fixes in 4/4 PRs regardless of implementer model — never skip it.

Live/eval invocations: live API probe = 'source ~/.config/bugbot/env && LLM_LIVE_BASE_URL=https://api.minimax.io/v1 LLM_LIVE_MODEL=MiniMax-M3 LLM_LIVE_API_KEY=$MINIMAX_API_KEY go test -tags live -run TestLive -v ./internal/llm/'. Corpus recorder (REAL spend, ~15min/3 cases): same env, 'go test -tags record -run TestRecordCorpus -v ./internal/eval/' writes internal/eval/testdata/recorded/. Offline replay: 'go test -run TestRecordedCorpusReplay ./internal/eval/' or 'bugbot eval --recorded'. Scripted gate: 'bugbot eval'. Calibration config recipe (old one was in /tmp, ephemeral): provider type openai-compatible + base_url + api_key_env MINIMAX_API_KEY, roles all MiniMax-M3, scope scan.include tightly and exclude **/*_test.go — budget overshoot (bugbot-zrr) means real runs can exceed per_cycle_tokens ~2.7x until fixed.

MiniMax API key (user-provided coding-plan key, OK for Bugbot use) lives in ~/.config/bugbot/env (chmod 600, outside git) as 'export MINIMAX_API_KEY=...'. Source it before live runs. Endpoint: https://api.minimax.io/v1 (OpenAI-compatible; /v1/models works). Models: MiniMax-M3 (validated: clean tool-calling, <think> blocks in content), plus M2.x family. Never put the key in bugbot.yaml or git — config references it via api_key_env.

Git workflow on this repo (github.com/dpoage/bugbot, default branch main): push feature branch, 'gh pr create', squash-merge via 'gh pr merge N --squash --delete-branch' (user-authorized standing); after merge 'git fetch && git rebase origin/main' on main; beads-only commits may push directly to main. SCARS: (1) NEVER 'git add -A' for a WIP commit on a feature branch — it captures .beads/issues.jsonl and guarantees a merge conflict whose wrong resolution can regress bead state on main (PR 15; recovery = 'bd export -o .beads/issues.jsonl' since Dolt is source of truth). (2) In a REBASE, --theirs = the branch being replayed, --ours = the base — backwards from merge. (3) Force-push is blocked by the permission classifier; prefer merging origin/main into the branch over rebase when the branch is already on the remote. (4) 'git worktree remove' BEFORE deleting its branch. (5) Long &&-chains with gh/git mix hide which step ran — keep merge sequences to short commands. CI = build/vet/test + golangci-lint v2; run locally exactly as CI: 'go run github.com/golangci/golangci-lint/v2/cmd/golangci-lint@latest run' (config tags integration,live,record).

Host environment: podman 5.8.2 (NO docker daemon) — sandbox uses CLI backend, integration tests 'go test -tags integration ./internal/sandbox/ ./internal/repro/' (~25s, images alpine + golang:1.26-alpine already pulled). Go toolchain go1.26.4 custom build; go.mod says 1.26.4. MiniMax-M3 operational notes: spend is input-dominated ~130:1 (no prompt caching on openai-compatible — biggest cost lever, see bugbot-zrr); finder agents on >10-file chunks run 12-30min and exhaust iterations/think-tokens ending with unparseable output (bugbot-210 is the fix; until then keep scan scopes tiny and expect 'No findings' to possibly mean 'finders failed' — check the skip/error lines).

Session bootstrap: 'bd ready' is the queue. Done through 2026-06-10 (PRs 4-14): infra+calibration prereqs (4-8), cross-lens dedup (9), PR review mode (10), verifier sandbox-exec (11), leads blackboard (12), GH issue publishing (13, bugbot-dx1), patch-prover w/ tier-0 + language generalization (14, bugbot-n8f). PARALLEL orchestration validated on 13+14: two Sonnet implementers in separate worktrees with pre-assigned migration numbers (005/006 — migrate.go tolerates the temporary gap) and explicit disjoint-file guardrails in prompts; merge sequentially, second branch rebases with only config.go union conflicts. Real-podman integration tests ('go test -tags integration ./internal/repro/', ~30s, no LLM spend) are cheap end-to-end verification for sandbox features — run them when touching repro/sandbox. LIVE-VALIDATION DEBT (fold into next calibration run): sandbox-exec refuters, leads posting, publish against a real repo, patch-prover with a real model. Remaining queue: KV memory bugbot-dtq (design-heavy, poisoning mitigations — Opus or inline design), OAuth bugbot-dbg (needs live header research + ToS check, inline design), eval fixture hygiene bugbot-18t (re-record spend), debate verification bugbot-jou, git churn bugbot-sro, plus P3s (SARIF export, lens generation, doc-contract lens, LSP robustness).
